edges.note: Anthropic's evaluator-optimizer and Microsoft's maker-checker loop describe the same generator+critic loop pattern.
Anthropic's Routing is static dispatch by a classifier; Microsoft's Handoff is dynamic peer-to-peer transfer between active agents. Related but not identical.
Both feature a central planner that dynamically decomposes work. Magentic adds an explicit task ledger and tool use against external systems.
Both fan-out then fan-in; aggregation pattern in cloud, ensemble pattern in AI.
RAG and Weng's long-term memory both use external vector stores with MIPS retrieval.
Evaluator-optimizer loops require a working evaluator, i.e., evals.
ReAct's Thought/Action/Observation loop is the core mechanism of an autonomous agent.
Both rely on self/peer critique to iteratively improve outputs.

cross_topic_patterns.name: Caching
cross_topic_patterns.note: Same underlying idea; LLM caching adds semantic-similarity and pre-computation variants.

cross_topic_patterns.name: Routing
cross_topic_patterns.note: Cloud routing is deterministic; LLM routing/handoff can be classifier- or agent-driven.

cross_topic_patterns.name: Rate Limiting / Throttling
cross_topic_patterns.note: Relevant to LLM apps too — Anthropic emphasizes iteration limits for agents (effectively a rate guard).

cross_topic_patterns.name: Retry + Circuit Breaker
cross_topic_patterns.note: Microsoft's AI agent orchestration doc explicitly recommends timeout/retry/circuit-breaker for agent dependencies.
